title: Diagnosed with peptic ulcer disease
description: Patient was diagnosed with peptic ulcer disease.

title: Peptic ulcer disease resolved with cimetidine
description: Peptic ulcer disease resolved after three months of cimetidine treatment.

title: Total abdominal hysterectomy with bilateral oophorectomy
description: Patient underwent total abdominal hysterectomy and bilateral oophorectomy for uterine fibroids.

title: Diagnosed with hypertension
description: Patient was diagnosed with hypertension and started on an unknown medication.

title: Bunionectomy performed
description: Patient underwent bunionectomy.

title: Stopped antihypertensive medication
description: Patient stopped her blood pressure medication after six months because it caused drowsiness.

title: Initial exertional chest pain episode
description: About one week before admission, patient developed abrupt dull aching left parasternal chest pain radiating to the neck while gardening after about 45 minutes of work; associated shortness of breath, lasted 5 to 10 minutes, and resolved with rest in a cool area.

title: Second chest pain episode while walking dog
description: Three days before admission, patient had a 15-minute episode of chest pain similar in quality and location to the first episode while walking her dog; it resolved with rest.

title: Nitrate therapy planned
description: Plan recommended starting nitrates to reduce risk of occlusion, treat chest pain symptoms, and help lower blood pressure.

title: Aspirin therapy planned
description: Plan recommended starting a platelet inhibitor such as aspirin to decrease myocardial infarction risk.

title: Diuretic therapy planned for dyspnea and volume overload
description: Plan recommended starting diuretics for dyspnea thought most likely due to volume overload; this was also expected to help blood pressure.

title: Chest pain assessed as likely angina
description: Assessment concluded that the exertional substernal chest pain was most suggestive of ischemic cardiac pain consistent with angina pectoris.

title: Angina considered most likely diagnosis
description: Assessment concluded that the chest pain was most suggestive of ischemic cardiac pain consistent with angina pectoris.

title: Unstable angina suspected
description: Because symptoms were increasing and pain occurred at rest, the presentation was considered consistent with unstable angina and hospitalization was indicated.

title: Plan for telemetry admission
description: Plan was to admit the patient to the telemetry floor for careful monitoring for increased chest pain and possible impending myocardial infarction.

title: Aspirin and nitrates planned
description: Plan was to start platelet inhibition with aspirin and begin nitrates to reduce occlusion risk, relieve pain, and help lower blood pressure.

title: Hospitalization deemed indicated
description: Assessment stated that hospitalization was indicated due to suspected unstable angina.

title: Cardiac catheterization planned
description: Plan was to schedule cardiac catheterization because of the high pretest probability of coronary disease.

title: Morphine considered for prolonged nitrate-unresponsive pain
description: If prolonged pain did not respond to nitrates, an analgesic such as morphine was considered.

title: Diuretics planned for dyspnea
description: Plan was to begin diuretics for dyspnea thought to be secondary to volume overload, which could also help treat hypertension.

title: Ventriculogram planned with catheterization
description: Plan included a ventriculogram at the time of catheterization to assess cardiac size and wall motion abnormalities.

title: Possible renovascular hypertension considered
description: Recent hypertension combined with an abdominal bruit raised concern for renal artery atherosclerotic disease causing renovascular hypertension.

title: Possible aortic stenosis considered
description: The systolic murmur radiating to the neck raised the possibility of significant valvular disease, specifically aortic stenosis.

title: Morphine planned if pain persists
description: For prolonged pain unresponsive to nitrates, morphine was planned as an analgesic option.
